# FM module FamilyRail primitive: era-attribute data-binding (ADR 0032 Â§2.3.3)

## Context

B-split of #14593 (horizon map v2, group B): the family rail as its own one-PR leaf â€” the visually smallest, contractually deepest primitive: it renders the resident's CURRENT episode family and must re-render in place on a family switch (same resident, ADR 0032 Â§2.3.3's live fixture).

## The Fix

The per-view triplet inside the agentos FM module (mount per #14631/#14632): (1) the FamilyRail component/mixin â€” a `--fm-family-*`-keyed left-border binding on a data field (era attribute; `harnessType`-derived proxy declared-as-such until #11318 lands); (2) unit specs â€” keyâ†’token mapping for all four families, unknown-key â†’ neutral + `unclassified` badge, rebind-in-place fixture (instance stable across a family change); (3) NL-verifiable render row.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Rail binds a data field, never a constant; rebind re-renders in place (the Â§2.3.3 fixture â€” instance id stable).
- [ ] All four family tokens + the neutral degrade render (CARD-CONTRACT rows).
- [ ] Tokens only (zero literal colors); unit specs (custom playwright config) + NL-verified row.

## Out of Scope

StateDot (#14593, narrowed) Â· HealthSwatch (sibling) Â· card composition (#14598).

- 2026-07-04T07:13:24Z @tobiu referenced in commit `1bc0708` - "feat(agentos): fleet cockpit StateDot session-state primitive (#14593) (#14700)

* feat(agentos): fleet cockpit state primitives â€” StateDot, FamilyRail, HealthSwatch (#14593)

The smallest visual primitives every fleet surface composes, built as Neo
functional components under apps/agentos/view/fleet/, consuming the #14578
--fm-* token layer (zero hand-rolled colors). The anti-lock-in render rules
are enforced once, here, instead of re-derived per consumer:

- StateDot: session state (ok/idle/wedged/limited/off) maps to --fm-state-*
  via a pure, exported stateToken() SSOT; a live config adds fm-live, whose
  pulse is gated behind prefers-reduced-motion in fleet-components.css (color
  carries the signal, motion is decoration). State is never identity.
- FamilyRail: family maps to --fm-family-* bound data-driven from the current
  episode's family key â€” a family swap re-renders the SAME resident.
- HealthSwatch: the health-summary legend entry, reusing stateToken as the
  one source of truth for state color.

fleet-components.css carries geometry + the reduced-motion-gated keyframes;
index.html links it beside tokens.css. 7 unit tests green: state/family/label
mappings, the live->fm-live config path, unknown->off fallback, and the
family-swap-same-resident id-stability.

- 2026-07-04T08:43:59Z @neo-opus-vega assigned to @neo-opus-vega
- 2026-07-04T08:51:56Z @neo-opus-vega cross-referenced by PR #14722
- 2026-07-04T09:20:35Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `5c3b234` - "fix(agentos): familyToken guards the closed set against prototype-shaped keys (#14635)

@neo-fable's #14722 pre-review reproduced it: familyToken used FAMILY_TOKEN[k]
|| fallback, so a prototype-shaped key (toString / constructor / __proto__)
returned the inherited Object.prototype member (truthy) instead of the neutral
fallback. Delegated to the existing isKnownFamily() hasOwn check so the closed
set stays closed. Regression assertions added (familyToken + isKnownFamily on
toString/constructor/__proto__). 4/4 green.

Systemic: the same MAP[k] || pattern is in kindToken/kindLabel (#14701),
stateLabel (#14726), and stateToken (StateDot, merged) â€” hardening those in
their PRs + a StateDot follow-up."
- 2026-07-04T09:47:01Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `9eb2c4f` - "feat(agentos): fleet cockpit FamilyRail primitive â€” data-driven, unclassified-safe (#14635)

The family-accent rail split out of the #14593 triplet per review, built to
its own full ACs (the versions bundled in the narrowed #14700 under-satisfied
them):

- family binds DATA-DRIVEN from the current-era key via a pure familyToken()
  resolver; a family swap re-renders the rail in place for the SAME resident
  (rebind-same-instance fixture), never forks a new self.
- unknown / absent family degrades to the NEUTRAL --fm-state-off token (never
  silently to human) + an fm-family-unclassified marker (a hatched neutral
  rail) â€” the render marks 'no recognized family' rather than guessing one.
- the family key is a harnessType-derived proxy, declared as such until the
  identity-state schema lands the first-class era attribute.

fleet-components.css gains the rail geometry + the unclassified hatch. 4 unit
tests green: family->token (known + unknown->neutral, not human), isKnownFamily,
the --fm-rail binding + unclassified marker, and the rebind-same-instance swap."

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-07-04T09:48:55Z

**Close-target annotation** (from @neo-gpt's evidence RA on PR #14722): AC#3's **NL-verified render row** is a documented residual, deferred to the #14598 AgentCard composer mount.

FamilyRail is a mount-less primitive â€” it can't be NL-rendered standalone without duplicating the composer's mount (the NL bridge currently reports `connected:false` / zero sessions), so the in-app render proof lands post-merge at composition, matching the merged StateDot #14700's deferral.

The other AC clauses are **met**: tokens-only / zero literal colors, and unit specs (4/4 green â€” keyâ†’token for all four families, unknown/absent â†’ neutral **not** human incl. prototype-shaped keys, `unclassified` marker, rebind-same-instance fixture). PR #14722 `Resolves` this on the StateDot precedent, with the NL render row carried as an explicit post-merge validation item.
